name: Georgia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities (DBHDD)
description: State agency providing services for individuals with developmental disabilities, mental health needs, and addictive diseases throughout Georgia.

name: Georgia Early Intervention (Babies Can't Wait)
description: Part C early intervention program serving infants and toddlers birth to age 3 with developmental delays or disabilities and their families.

name: The Arc Georgia
description: Statewide advocacy organization for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities providing information, referral, and community support.

name: Special Olympics Georgia
description: Year-round sports training and competition programs for individuals with intellectual disabilities throughout Georgia.

name: Georgia Advocacy Office
description: Federally designated protection and advocacy agency providing legal advocacy and rights protection for Georgians with disabilities.
address: One CNN Center, Suite 850, Atlanta, GA 30303

name: Georgia Respite Coalition
description: Statewide respite care network providing referrals and resources for family caregivers of children and adults with disabilities.

name: Camp Dream (Disability Resource, Education, and Advocacy Movement)
description: Camp and recreation programs for children and adults with disabilities providing inclusive outdoor experiences and social engagement.

name: Tools for Life - Georgia Assistive Technology Act Program
description: Georgia's assistive technology program at Georgia Tech providing device demonstrations, equipment loans, and AT training statewide.

name: Georgia ABLE Account (STABLE)
description: Tax-advantaged savings accounts for individuals with disabilities to save without losing eligibility for public benefits like Medicaid and SSI.

name: Georgia Medicaid Waiver Programs (NOW/COMP)
description: Home and community-based Medicaid waivers (New Options Waiver and Comprehensive Supports Waiver) for individuals with developmental disabilities.

name: MARTA Mobility Paratransit (Atlanta)
description: Paratransit transportation service for individuals with disabilities in the metro Atlanta area who cannot use fixed-route transit.

name: Georgia Department of Community Affairs - Accessible Housing
description: State housing agency providing resources for accessible and affordable housing for individuals with disabilities throughout Georgia.

name: Emory Developmental Pediatrics
description: Developmental pediatrics program at Emory University and Children's Healthcare of Atlanta providing comprehensive evaluations and care.

name: Easter Seals North Georgia Pediatric Therapy
description: Pediatric therapy services including physical, occupational, and speech therapy for children with disabilities in North Georgia.

name: Georgia Vocational Rehabilitation Agency (GVRA) - Employment
description: State vocational rehabilitation program providing job training, placement, and employment support for Georgians with disabilities.

name: BlazeSports America
description: Adaptive sports organization born from the 1996 Atlanta Paralympic Games, providing year-round sports programs for youth and adults with physical disabilities.
